title: BEAM Therapeutics Q2 2025 Clinical Development Progress
text: BEAM Therapeutics reports on 2025 pipeline progress in key clinical programs. Risto-cel (BEAM-101) for Sickle Cell Disease: BEAM dosed 30 patients in the BEACON trial, marking an important milestone on path to BLA filing with expected submission as early as year-end 2026. BEAM-302 for Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency: With 17 patients dosed in Phase 1/2 trial, BEAM-302 continues demonstrating durable correction of disease-causing mutation and restoration of AAT physiology. Expanded dose exploration in Part A and enrollment in Part B with patients having mild to moderate liver disease underway. Alignment reached with FDA on potential accelerated approval pathway based on biomarker endpoints. BEAM-103 ESCAPE Platform: First subject dosed in Phase 1 healthy volunteer clinical trial of BEAM-103, an anti-CD117 monoclonal antibody developed as part of ESCAPE platform. BEAM-301 for Glycogen Storage Disease Ia: Company continues dosing in Phase 1/2 clinical trial. BEAM-304 for Phenylketonuria: IND filing anticipated in 2026. Financial position: $1.25 billion in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of December 31, 2025.
